title: Samantha and the Sky Dolphins
author: Dady Fredy
subtitle: A Bedtime Lullaby by Dady Fredy
dedication: For Samantha — born to swim the sky.

lines: Swim through the sky…
Fly through the sea…
Sky dolphins carry
Samantha and me.

pages.title: Samantha and the Sky Dolphins
pages.subtitle: A Bedtime Lullaby by Dady Fredy
pages.dedication: For Samantha — born to swim the sky.

lines: The moon hung low, the tide rolled in—
A flash of silver, a gleaming fin!
Dady Fredy laughed and whispered then,
"Samantha, look — they're here again."
They rose from the ocean on silver-blue wings,
Barnacles bright, coral-bead rings.
They leapt through the clouds like ribbons of light,
And called to Samantha, "Come fly with us tonight!"
She climbed on the biggest — his name was Bayani,
Whose skin shimmered blue like the seas near Subani.
Dady Fredy rode next on a dolphin called Star,
Who hummed a soft song as they sailed afar.
A flamingo in pajamas waved from a cloud,
A family of turtles sang sleepy and loud.
Three parrots played flutes of sugarcane reed,
While fireflies lit lanterns and scattered bright seed.
They dove through a rainbow that arched the night,
Where jellyfish glowed in pink, purple, and white.
An elephant floated on pillows of breeze,
And blew them a trumpet of honey and cheese.
"Look, Dady!" Samantha laughed, holding on tight,
As a whale made of moonbeams swam into sight.
He opened his mouth and the stars tumbled out—
Ten thousand gold wishes that scattered about.
The dolphins dove deeper through cinnamon air,
Past tigers in hammocks and one dancing bear.
A penguin read a bedtime book, too,
And waved at Samantha, "This chapter's for you!"
They looped past the planets and waved to the moon,
Who hummed them a soft, half-sleepy tune.
The owls played their cellos, the crickets kept time,
And everything moved to a slow, dreamy rhyme.
But even sky dolphins must rest from their flight,
And even brave dreamers must sleep through the night.
So Bayani glided down low through the air,
With sea-spray like diamonds caught soft in their hair.
They landed on water as still as a dream,
Where the moon poured a golden, shimmering stream.
The dolphins sang softly — a lullaby hymn,
As stars filled the ocean right up to the brim.
And Dady Fredy held Samantha so tight,
And whispered the words he says every night:
"Wherever you swim, anak… wherever you fly—
I'll be the dolphin right there in your sky."
So close your bright eyes now, my Samantha, my love,
The dolphins are sleeping on clouds up above.
The animals dream and the oceans are still—
And Dady Fredy loves you… he always, always will.
Goodnight, Samantha.
Goodnight, anak.
